title: Python 3.14, pygame-ce 2.5.7, SDL, Initialization, and the Main Loop · Traceback Repair Bay
summary: A preserved game trace contains one plausible state, timing, input, vector, rendering, collision, ownership, audio, accessibility, packaging, or release defect; locate the first failed invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in owned runtime and minimal event-update-render loop and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Pin Python 3.14 and released pygame-ce 2.5.7 as a mutually supported stack while distinguishing package name from import name.
Explain how pygame calls into SDL and identify display, event, input, audio, timer, filesystem, and platform boundaries.
Initialize only required modules, inspect failures, create the display before dependent conversions, and preserve causal diagnostics.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Inspect the failure record · Debug Runtime Choice
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug Asteroids team handles case 6727 during a headless continuous-integration replay. Build deterministic Python evidence to own initialization, the event-render loop, quit handling, and cleanup under a versioned Python and SDL boundary; keep every critical cue available through more than color or sound and every action remappable without timing precision; then replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Pin Python 3.14 and released pygame-ce 2.5.7 as a mutually supported stack while distinguishing package name from import name.

Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Handle window and program exit through one owned stop path that releases resources and returns a status.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Quit Cleanup
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: asteroids-quit-cleanup-1pjn1i" that uses case 1858 to own initialization, the event-render loop, quit handling, and cleanup under a versioned Python and SDL boundary. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native pygame-ce, SDL, window, controller, mixer, filesystem, package, GPU, installer, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, released pygame-ce 2.5.7, fixed and headless SDL fixtures, real devices, licensed assets, accessibility playtests, profiling, clean installations, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Quit Cleanup.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Quit Cleanup into observable performance evidence. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Quit Cleanup, keep Quit Cleanup connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-quit-cleanup-1pjn1i
# Competency: asteroids-quit-cleanup.
# Case 1858: a keyboard-only community arcade night.
# Operating constraint: retain player goal, revision, Python, pygame-ce, SDL, fixture, seed, input trace, viewport, and result identity.
# Changed case: lose window focus while thrust and fire are held, then prove both actions release safely.
def evidence_asteroids_quit_cleanup_1pjn1i(python_minor: int = 14, pygame_ce_minor: int = 7, initialized_modules: int = 5, quit_handled: bool = True, cleanup_called: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1pjn1i = "1pjn1i-i1njp1-1858"
    assert evidence_variant_1pjn1i
    evidence_axes_1pjn1i = (
        "player1pjn1i",
        "seed1858",
        "inputi1njp1",
        "frame1pjn1i1858",
        "state18581pjn1i",
        "playtesti1njp11858",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1pjn1i)) == 6
    if python_minor < 14 or pygame_ce_minor < 7:
        return False, python_minor, "unsupported-runtime"
    if initialized_modules < 3:
        return False, initialized_modules, "partial-init"
    if not quit_handled:
        return False, initialized_modules, "missing-quit"
    if not cleanup_called:
        return False, initialized_modules, "missing-cleanup"
    assert initialized_modules >= 3
    return True, initialized_modules, "owned-runtime"
